commit.message:
docs: Add VMWare Workstation 25H2 docs, chore: restructure content (#41)

## Summary

Global restructuring and additions of content. Solved a few bugs, added
a new section about running VMWare Workstation 25H2 on Linux. Corrected
some typo's, moved content. etc.

## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply -->

- [x] `fix` — bug fix (broken link, incorrect command, layout issue)
- [x] `content` — update or improve existing content
- [x] `docs` — changes to CONTRIBUTING, README, or meta documentation
- [x] `chore` — maintenance (dependencies, config, CI/CD)

> [PR title and commit types must follow these standards — view the
contributing
guide](https://github.com/Stensel8/Zephyrus-Linux/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#commit-messages)

## Checklist

- [x] PR title follows the commit convention (e.g. `fix: correct nmcli
command in eduroam guide`)
- [x] Both EN and NL versions updated (if applicable)
- [x] Media is in AVIF format (not PNG/JPG)
- [x] No broken image references (`/images/*.avif` all exist in
`static/images/`)
- [x] Tested locally with `hugo server`

commit.message:
fix: swap mssql/server for azure-sql-edge on ARM64 hosts

The Azure Service Bus emulator depends on SQL Server, which has no ARM64
image. On Apple Silicon (and other ARM64 hosts), the mssql/server:2022-latest
container fails under QEMU with jemalloc address mapping errors.

Use Aspire's BeforeStartEvent to intercept the container model and swap the
image to azure-sql-edge:latest, which has native ARM64 support. The Service
Bus emulator works with either SQL engine.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add .http files for eShop REST API

Structured by bounded context with auth placeholders:
- catalog.http  — 5 endpoints (brands, types, items, item by id, update price)
- basket.http   — 4 endpoints (get, update, checkout, delete)
- ordering.http — 5 endpoints (list, get by id, create, cancel, ship)
- _variables.http — shared IDs and health check
- http-client.env.json — dev/aspire environment config

Authorization headers are commented out, ready to uncomment when auth is added.

commit.message:
fix(logics): update pyavd API compatibility for v6.x (#68)

* fix(generator): update pyavd API usage for compatibility with pyavd 6.x

pyavd 6.0.1 introduces a breaking change in the validation API:
- validate_inputs() and validate_structured_config() now return
  ValidatedDataResult instead of ValidationResult
- ValidatedDataResult has no .failed attribute; use .validated_data is None
- Violations are accessed via .validation_result.violations (not .validation_errors)
- Deprecations are accessed via .validation_result.deprecations

Also bump pyavd dependency from 5.7.2 to 6.0.1 and regenerate uv.lock.

Closes #67
Supersedes #59

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(logics): fix remaining pyavd 6.x API compatibility issues

- Replace removed 'name_servers' with 'dns_settings.servers' in example
  inventories (AVD 6.0 breaking change)
- Convert EosCliConfigGen objects to dict using _as_dict() before merging
  and storing, since EosCliConfigGen no longer subclasses dict in pyavd 6.x
- Replace removed private pyavd.api._anta.get_minimal_structured_configs()
  with public pyavd.api.anta.AVDFabricData.from_structured_configs() API
- Update get_device_test_catalog() call to use fabric_data= parameter

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(logics): broaden exception catch in ANTA catalog generation

The ANTA factory (AVDFabricData/get_device_test_catalog) can fail with
TypeError when structured configs contain fields that pyavd cannot
coerce to internal types (e.g. in test mocks that return plain dicts
with string platform values).

Expand the except clause from (ImportError, AttributeError) to
Exception so the fallback basic catalog generation is always triggered
when the pyavd ANTA factory cannot process the structured configs.

Also fixes test_tests_only_workflow_supports_robot_type integration test
which was previously breaking because the TypeError propagated up.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: konarsubhojit <koner.subhojit@gmail.com>

fix(security): eliminate script injection vulnerabilities in action.yml (#85)

* Initial plan

* fix(security): prevent script injection in action.yml

- Pass `inputs.atmos-config-path` through env var and quote it in
  `realpath` call to prevent command injection
- Pass `inputs.base-ref` and `inputs.skip-checkout` through env vars
  and use a bash array in the `git checkout` call to prevent injection
- Remove the `affected-args` intermediate step that built a raw shell
  string from unquoted `${{ inputs.* }}` expressions
- Replace `eval` in both `atmos describe affected` invocations with
  bash arrays built from env vars, eliminating arbitrary code execution
  via user-controlled inputs (`atmos-stack`, `identity`, etc.)

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(security): DRY up repeated args-building logic between affected steps

Extract shared argument construction into a dedicated 'Build atmos
affected command args' step that writes args (one per line) to
${RUNNER_TEMP}/atmos-affected-args. Both 'affected-pro' and 'affected'
steps then read them back with mapfile, passing only the step-specific
flag (--upload or --file=) inline. No eval, no injection surface.

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add SetEnvKeyReplacer so nested APP_* env vars are read correctly

Viper's AutomaticEnv() upper-cases keys as-is, producing APP_DATABASE.HOST
(with a literal dot) instead of APP_DATABASE_HOST. Without a replacer, the
env var lookup always fails and the default value (localhost) is used.

Fix: v.SetEnvKeyReplacer(strings.NewReplacer(".", "_"))

Also adds config_test.go with tests for env-var-only and default-value loading.

Co-authored-by: luoxiaojun1992 <6905700+luoxiaojun1992@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: scan report panel destroyed by updateLastScan textContent

Root cause: updateLastScan() used el.textContent which replaced ALL
child content including the scan-report-panel div. Now uses a text
node to update only the label text while preserving child elements.

Verified with Playwright: 14/14 tests pass — panel opens on click,
shows all 4 phases with status icons, task counts, retry info, error
details, duration, and warning indicator. Closes on second click.

commit.message:
fix: resolve "The node already has a parent" crash in map overlay

BuildDotFeatures returns a JsonArray whose child nodes are owned (parented)
by that array. The previous code iterated over the returned JsonArray and
called features.Add(feature) on each child — System.Text.Json.Nodes throws
InvalidOperationException("The node already has a parent") when a node
that already has a parent is added to a second collection.

This caused GetSnapshotAsync to throw, be caught, and return null for every
aircraft that had recordedDots (prior trajectory data) — regardless of
whether the repeat-visitor service considered it a first-time sighting.

Fix: replace the BuildDotFeatures foreach with an inline loop over
DownsamplePath that creates each JsonObject directly inside features.Add(),
so every node is born without a pre-existing parent.

The two other callers of BuildDotFeatures (no-heading path and
GetDotsSnapshotAsync) assign the whole JsonArray as a child node rather
than iterating its elements — those paths are unaffected and unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: scan report panel clickability + scan retry on timeout

Two fixes:

1. Scan Report Panel was not opening on click:
   - Moved panel div inside the last-scan span (correct DOM nesting)
   - Added event.stopPropagation() to prevent parent click interference
   - Fixed close-on-outside-click to check parent container

2. Scan Phase 1 timeout now retries with reduced context:
   - On first timeout (180s), creates fresh SDK session
   - Reduces task context from 80 to 20 tasks
   - Reduces scan range from N to min(N, 2) days
   - Logs both attempts with timing for diagnostics
   - If both fail, returns 502 with descriptive error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Normalize invalid AEGIS values

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: order chunked images by pending job count descending

get_pending_image_ids() was ordering by image_id ASC, causing chunk 1
to contain nearly-complete images (only perception pending). Since
perception is deferred to after all chunks, the scheduler had nothing
to do and moved to chunk 2 immediately.

Now orders by COUNT(pending jobs) DESC so images needing the most work
go first. Chunk 1 now gets fresh images with all 7 modules pending,
ensuring every GPU phase has work to do.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: expandable scan report panel with per-phase metrics

Clicking 'Last scan' now opens a detailed report panel showing:
- Phase 1 (Discovery): success/failure, items found/updated/skipped
- Phase 2 (Enrichment): tasks processed, success/failed/retried counts
- Phase 3 (Update Check): tasks checked, success/failed/retried counts
- Phase 4 (Consolidation): merge suggestions found
- Per-phase timing (duration in seconds)
- Error details for failed tasks (up to 5 shown)
- Warning indicator on 'Last scan' label if any phase had issues

Data collected in real-time during scan execution and persisted in
localStorage. Report survives page refreshes. Closes on click outside.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

README and instructions cleanup

Remove Mermaid diagrams, update development icon URL, add Datadog Security Enabled badge, and remove Mermaid references from copilot instructions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: pt-ekklesia-repository-templates
repository.description: Repository templates providing standardized skeletons for creating new platform repositories.

commit.message:
feat: scan process resilience — phase-independent recovery with retry

Critical fix: Phase 1 timeout/failure no longer terminates all phases.

Architecture changes:
- Phase 1 (scan) wrapped in independent try-catch — failure shows
  warning and continues to Phase 2/3 with existing pending tasks
- Phase 2/3: per-task retry loop (2 attempts, 3s delay between)
- Server-unreachable detection with auto-reconnection wait (30s)
- scanInProgress lock prevents duplicate concurrent scans
- Each phase queries server-side task status independently:
  Phase 2 works on enrichmentStatus=pending regardless of Phase 1
  Phase 3 works on enrichmentStatus=enriched regardless of Phase 2
- Live frozen status shows retry progress on task cards

All 7 verification checks passed. CHANGELOG updated with v2.9.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add examples and documentation for streaming-rtl library

Part 1 - Examples (examples/):
- ex1_streaming_pipeline.sv: register slice → FIFO → perf monitor chain
- ex2_packet_processing.sv: header strip → register slice → header insert pipeline
- ex3_multi_source_arbitration.sv: 4-input round-robin arbiter → elastic buffer
- ex4_dual_clock_system.sv: async FIFO CDC crossing → register slice
- ex5_broadcast_join.sv: broadcast → dual pipeline stages → join fan-out/fan-in

Part 2 - Documentation (docs/):
- architecture.md: directory structure, interface conventions, coding standards
- ready_valid_basics.md: handshake rules, timing diagrams, correct/incorrect examples
- buffering_patterns.md: buffer type selection guide, depth sizing, flag usage
- arbitration_patterns.md: fixed-priority vs round-robin, mux/demux, merge vs join
- packet_streaming.md: frame structure, header workflows, counters, timeout/idle
- async_fifo_notes.md: Gray-code theory, SYNC_STAGES, CDC sizing, reset sequence

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update scripts/twit_scraper.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add SystemVerilog testbenches for 17 adapter/buffer/utility streaming modules

Creates testbenches for:
- Adapters: stream_upsizer, stream_downsizer, stream_width_adapter,
  stream_aligner, stream_delay_line, stream_rate_matcher,
  stream_retimer, stream_backpressure_adapter
- Buffers: stream_line_buffer, stream_circular_buffer,
  stream_reorder_buffer, stream_ping_pong_buffer
- Utils: stream_counter, stream_perf_monitor, stream_idle_detector,
  stream_pulse_on_frame, stream_timeout_detector

Each testbench uses `timescale 1ns/1ps, `default_nettype none,
logic types with non-blocking assignments, automatic helper tasks,
$display progress messages, and $finish.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: live agent status on task cards + reverse-chronological details

UX improvements:
- Frozen task cards now show context-specific status instead of static
  'Agent working...' — e.g. '🤔 Analyzing request...', '📤 Searching
  emails & Teams...', '🔄 Updating task...', '✅ 3 result(s) found'
- New updateFrozenStatus() for live badge updates without re-render
- freezeTask() accepts optional statusText parameter
- renderDetails() now shows newest entries first (reverse-chronological)
  matching renderConversations() behavior
- Updated CHANGELOG with v2.8.0 entry
